title: Bad Bunny Makes Grammy History with Album of the Year Win
text: Bad Bunny achieved a groundbreaking milestone at the 2026 Grammy Awards by becoming the first artist to win Album of the Year with a Spanish-language record. His album DeBÍ TiRAR MáS FOToS defeated strong competition including works by Kendrick Lamar, Tyler the Creator, and others. This victory marks a significant progression in the artist's Grammy trajectory. In 2023, his album Un Verano Sin Ti became the first Spanish-language LP nominated in the Album of the Year category, though it lost to Harry Styles' Harry's House. This year, Bad Bunny made additional history by becoming the first Latin music artist nominated for Album of the Year, Record of the Year, and Song of the Year simultaneously. Speech Highlights: During his acceptance remarks, Bad Bunny delivered most of his speech in Spanish, emphasizing Puerto Rico's achievements: "There's nothing we can't achieve." He expressed gratitude to God, the Academy, and those who supported his career throughout the years. He also used the platform to address broader themes, stating: "I want to dedicate this award to all the people that had to leave their homeland, their country to follow their dreams." Later in the evening, Bad Bunny won two additional awards: Best Global Musical Performance and Best Música Urbana Album, using the latter acceptance speech to criticize ICE enforcement actions.
